name: Rust SDK Port Pipeline
description: Multi-phase workflow for porting the LogixEdge C++ SDK to Rust. Developer implements on feature branches, QA validates with cargo build/test/fmt/clippy, merges to main on success.

ASSIGN.name: Task Assignment
ASSIGN.description: Manager routes the Rust port phase specification to the developer. The workflow engine handles routing mechanically.

IMPLEMENTING.name: Implementation
IMPLEMENTING.description: Developer implements the current Rust port phase on a feature branch from latest main.
IMPLEMENTING.prompt:
You are implementing a phase of the LogixEdge SDK Rust port.

You are ALREADY on branch dev/{{taskId}} -- the entry commands created it from main. Do NOT create branches or run git checkout.

**Context files to read first:**
- {{referenceDoc}} (integration strategies and codebase assessment)
- {{rustInstructions}} (Rust coding conventions for this project)
- core/cpp/include/logix/edge/ (C++ public API surface you are wrapping)

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Read the reference documents listed above
3. Execute each work item sequentially
4. After completing all items:
   - Run: {{fmtCommand}}
   - Run: {{buildCommand}}
   - Run: {{testCommand}}
   - Run: {{lintCommand}}
5. Fix ALL errors -- do not proceed with failures

**Rust conventions:**
- Every unsafe FFI call must have a // SAFETY: comment
- Implement Drop for every handle wrapper
- Include Rockwell Automation copyright header in every .rs file
- Use the non-templated raw-data callback overloads on IpcClient for the C shim

Do NOT call send_completion_report() or send_message() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions automatically.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT create a new branch. Do NOT run git checkout. The exit commands will commit and push automatically.
tasks: unit tests including round-trip tests for every public struct, with 80%+ line coverage
validation that all unit tests pass
IMPLEMENTING.decompositionPrompt: Place test-writing work items AFTER implementation items but BEFORE any final verification step. Each test work item should reference the specific module and public items to test. Read the reference documents before decomposing.

onEntryCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
onEntryCommands.reason: Remove stale lock files and abort any stuck operations.
Sync local main to latest origin/main.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout -b dev/{{taskId}} || git checkout dev/{{taskId}}
onEntryCommands.reason: Create the feature branch from main or switch to it if existing.

onExitCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true

params.message: Rust port phase pushed for task {{taskId}}

VALIDATING.name: QA Validation
VALIDATING.description: QA checks out the feature branch and runs cargo build, test, clippy, and fmt. Read-only -- QA must not modify source files.

onEntryCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
git checkout dev/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b dev/{{taskId}} origin/dev/{{taskId}}

params.message: QA validation completed for Rust port task {{taskId}}

REWORK.name: Rework
REWORK.description: Developer fixes QA-reported issues on the same feature branch.

onEntryCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true

onExitCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m '{{role}}: rework fix for {{taskId}}'

MERGING.name: Merge to Main
MERGING.description: Mechanical merge: rebase feature onto main, merge, push, delete branch.

onEntryCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
git checkout dev/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b dev/{{taskId}} origin/dev/{{taskId}}
git merge dev/{{taskId}} --no-ff -m 'Merge dev/{{taskId}}: Rust port - {{taskId}}'

params.message: Rust port phase merged to main for task {{taskId}}

DONE.name: Complete
DONE.description: Rust port phase complete. Code merged to main, feature branch deleted.

ESCALATED.name: Escalated
ESCALATED.description: Phase failed after retries. Requires human intervention.
